causes: Insufficient gas coverage or gas flow rate too low/high
Contaminated base metal (oil, rust, paint, moisture)
Wind or drafts blowing shielding gas away
Gas cylinder empty or regulator malfunction
Wrong shielding gas for the process
solutions: Check gas flow rate (typically 20-25 CFH for MIG)
Clean base metal thoroughly before welding (grind, wire brush, or solvent clean)
Clean or replace gas nozzle and contact tip
Block wind/drafts or use wind screens
Verify gas supply and check for leaks in gas hose
Reduce electrode stickout to 3/8" to 1/2"
Verify correct gas type (75/25 Ar/CO2 for MIG, 100% Argon for TIG)

causes: Voltage too high or too low
solutions: Adjust voltage — use the synergic settings as a starting point
Adjust wire feed speed to match voltage
Verify correct polarity (DCEP for MIG, DCEN for self-shielded flux-cored)
Maintain proper arc length (about 3/8")
Clean base metal before welding
Use correct gas (75/25 Ar/CO2 or 100% CO2 for MIG)

problem: Wire feed issues / wire not feeding smoothly
causes: Drive roll tension too loose or too tight
Wrong drive roll type for wire size
Contact tip worn or wrong size
solutions: Adjust drive roll tension — wire should slip with light finger pressure on the spool
Use knurled drive rolls for flux-cored wire, smooth/V-groove for solid wire
Replace liner if kinked; blow out with compressed air if clogged
Replace contact tip and ensure correct size for wire diameter
Adjust spool brake tension — spool should stop freely without unraveling
Cut out tangled wire, re-thread through drive rolls and liner

causes: Work clamp not connected or poor ground connection
solutions: Verify work clamp is securely attached to clean bare metal
Check LCD display and select correct welding process
Check MIG gun trigger cable connection
Wait for machine to cool down (thermal overload resets automatically)
Check input power — verify correct voltage (120V or 240V)
Trim wire flush with contact tip, check wire feed

causes: Wrong wire size for material thickness
solutions: Reduce amperage/wire feed speed
Increase travel speed
Use 0.023" or 0.030" wire for thin materials
Ensure tight fit-up with minimal gap
Use stitch (intermittent) welding technique

problem: Weld too cold / lack of fusion
solutions: Increase amperage/wire feed speed
Slow down travel speed
Reduce electrode stickout to 3/8" to 1/2"
Verify correct polarity for your process
